segments.title: Destroyers as fleet shield and spear
segments.summary: The opening frames 134 years of destroyer development around a persistent shield-and-spear role for fleets.
segments.body: Destroyers changed from torpedo-boat answers into modern surface combatants, but their basic fleet function remains defensive screening plus offensive striking power.

evidence.note: The opening says destroyers have existed for 134 years and describes their recurring task as both shield and spear for the fleet.
The transcript ties the destroyer story to the Whitehead torpedo, Jeune Ecole claims, and the recurring need for mixed fleets rather than only small or large ships.
The transcript describes flotilla defense, torpedo boats, torpedo-boat destroyers, and quick-firing guns as answers to smaller threats.
